description: Update pack config file loaders: new 5-element JSONL format [dataset_path(str), sample_idx, char_start, char_end, token_start_offset]; replace NPY loader with Parquet loader using load_mixed_dict_from_parquet from jsonl.py; update dispatch function. Add loader-level unit tests.
steps: Step 1: Update _load_pack_config_jsonl to parse 5-element slices [dataset_path(str), sample_idx, char_start, char_end, token_start_offset]; validate each slice has exactly 5 elements

Step 2: Delete _load_pack_config_npy; add _load_pack_config_parquet using load_mixed_dict_from_parquet from jsonl.py (reads boundaries np.ndarray, samples list[list[int]], paths list[str] from single .parquet file)
Step 3: Update _load_pack_config dispatch: .jsonl -> JSONL loader, .parquet -> Parquet loader
Step 4: Update test helpers in test_custom_pack_dataset.py: update _write_jsonl_pack for 5-element format; delete _write_npy_pack; add _write_parquet_pack using save_mixed_dict_to_parquet
Step 5: Write unit tests verifying JSONL and Parquet configs are loaded correctly (pack count, slice count, path/idx/char fields preserved) and run tests to pass

description: Rewrite CustomPackDataset __init__ and _validate_pack: build path->index mapping, remove skip strategy, new char-range validation, fix token total computation. Add validation-level unit tests.
steps: Step 1: Update CustomPackDataset.__init__: build path->index mapping from datasets list (using ds.path); remove 'skip' from short_pack_strategy and long_pack_strategy Literal types; fix _sample_num_tokens to use ds.num_tokens[i] directly (not double-indexed via ds.sampled)

Step 2: Update _validate_pack: new char-range validation (both -1 for DataItem is OK; else require char_start>=0 and char_end>char_start); remove all skip handling code; compute token total via dataset.num_tokens[s_idx]
Step 3: Update _FakeDataset in tests to support the new 5-element format and have a .path attribute for path->index mapping

Step 4: Write unit tests for validation: hard errors (invalid dataset_path, sample_idx out of range, invalid char range), short_pack_strategy='error'/'padding', long_pack_strategy='error'/'truncate'; run tests to pass

description: Rewrite CustomPackDataset __getitem__: remove token slicing, add DataItem vs LongTextDataItem consistency check, handle padding. Add getitem-level unit tests covering DataItem, LongTextDataItem and mixed cases.
steps: Step 1: Update __getitem__: remove token slicing (input_ids[t_start:t_end]); add consistency check - for char_start==-1 verify 'char_start' not in item (plain DataItem); for char_start!=-1 verify item is LongTextDataItem with matching char_start/char_end/token_start_offset; apply padding for short_pack_strategy='padding'

Step 2: Write unit test for DataItem case (char_start==-1) with both JSONL and Parquet configs: verify __getitem__ returns correct DataItem list with matching input_ids/labels/num_tokens; run tests to pass
Step 3: Write unit test for LongTextDataItem case (char_start/char_end/token_start_offset set): verify __getitem__ consistency check passes and returns correct LongTextDataItem; run tests to pass

Step 4: Write unit test for mixed DataItem and LongTextDataItem slices in same pack; write unit test that consistency check raises an error when returned item fields do not match pack config; run all tests to pass

description: DataloaderConfig.build enforces JsonlDataset settings when pack_level='custom': sample_ratio=1.0, enable_sequential_sampler=True, disable_filter=True. Requires adding disable_filter parameter to JsonlDataset and DatasetConfig. Add unit tests.
steps: Step 1: Add disable_filter: bool = False parameter to JsonlDataset.__init__; when True, skip all filter logic (num_tokens==0 filter and max_length filter) so _sampled is not reduced
Step 2: Add disable_filter: bool = False field to DatasetConfig; pass it to JsonlDataset in DatasetConfig.build()

Step 3: In DataloaderConfig.build, when pack_level=='custom', force each DatasetConfig to sample_ratio=1.0, enable_sequential_sampler=True, disable_filter=True before calling build_datasets; log a warning if any value was overridden from a non-default setting

Step 4: Write unit test verifying disable_filter=True on JsonlDataset skips all filters; write unit test verifying DataloaderConfig with pack_level='custom' forces the three settings regardless of what was configured; run tests to pass

description: Upgrade pack config slice from 5-tuple to 6-tuple by adding token_end_offset: [dataset_path, sample_idx, char_start, char_end, token_start_offset, token_end_offset]. Update all loaders, _PackSlice/_ValidatedSlice types, validation logic, and unit tests.
steps: Step 1: Update _PackSlice to 6-element tuple; update _load_pack_config_jsonl to require exactly 6 elements per slice and parse token_end_offset
Step 2: Update _load_pack_config_parquet to read and decode the 6th element (token_end_offset) from each sample entry
Step 3: Update _ValidatedSlice to include token_end_offset; update _validate_pack to validate token_end_offset > token_start_offset >= 0, and propagate token_end_offset into slices
Step 4: Update test helpers _write_jsonl_pack and _write_parquet_pack to emit 6-element slices; update all existing test fixtures
Step 5: Write/update unit tests verifying loaders parse token_end_offset correctly and validation rejects token_end_offset <= token_start_offset; run tests to pass

description: Rewrite CustomPackDataset.__getitem__ to distinguish two TokenizeFn cases: for plain TokenizeFn (char_start==-1) apply input_ids[token_start_offset:token_end_offset] truncation; for LongTextTokenizeFn the item is pre-truncated so only validate.
steps: Step 1: For char_start==-1 (plain TokenizeFn): after verifying item is plain DataItem, truncate via input_ids[token_start_offset:token_end_offset] instead of relying on max_tokens from _ValidatedSlice
Step 2: For char_start!=-1 (LongTextTokenizeFn): keep existing field-match validation; skip manual truncation since item is pre-truncated at tokenize time
Step 3: Write unit test for plain TokenizeFn case verifying truncation via token_start_offset:token_end_offset is applied; run tests to pass
Step 4: Write unit test for LongTextTokenizeFn case verifying no extra truncation occurs; run all tests to pass

description: Switch pack_config_path to NPY directory format (boundaries.npy, samples.npy, paths.npy). Add load_config(path, mmap=True) function. Replace Python-loop _validate_pack with vectorized numpy validation. Move long_pack_strategy='truncate' handling from __init__ to __getitem__. Update __getitem__ to do segment reads on mmap'd arrays.
steps: Step 1: Add load_config(path: str, mmap: bool = True) -> dict[str, np.ndarray] that loads boundaries.npy (int64), samples.npy (int64, shape Nx6), paths.npy (object) from the given directory; use np.load(mmap_mode='r') for int64 arrays when mmap=True; always load paths.npy in full

Step 2: Rewrite CustomPackDataset.__init__: call load_config; store _boundaries and _samples as mmap'd arrays and _paths as a loaded array; build _path_to_ds_idx from _paths; remove the old _load_pack_config / raw_packs / pack_infos list machinery

Step 3: Replace _validate_pack Python loop with vectorized numpy validation: structural checks (boundaries[-1]==len(samples), shape[1]==6); field checks (path_id range, token_start_offset>=0, token_end_offset>token_start_offset, char constraints); for short_pack_strategy='error' and long_pack_strategy='error' use np.add.reduceat on token lengths to compute per-pack sums and raise if any pack total != pack_max_length

Step 4: Update __getitem__(i): read row slice _samples[_boundaries[i]:_boundaries[i+1]] (segment read); for long_pack_strategy='truncate' compute running token sum and stop at pack_max_length instead of using pre-computed max_tokens; update __len__ to len(_boundaries)-1
Step 5: Update test helpers to write NPY directory format (write_npy_pack_dir); update all existing test fixtures to use the new format; run all existing tests to pass

description: Stress test CustomPackDataset at 2-billion-slice scale with an 8-process distributed test. Mock datasets to isolate CustomPackDataset performance. Measure init time, __getitem__ time, and memory usage (tracemalloc for Python objects + psutil RSS for total process memory).
steps: Step 1: Write a helper generate_stress_pack_config(num_slices, pack_max_length, out_dir) that generates boundaries.npy, samples.npy, paths.npy in NPY directory format; token length per slice drawn uniformly from [200, 16000]; each pack filled greedily until pack_max_length is reached; total slices ~2 billion (adjust num_packs so total_slices ≈ 2e9)

Step 2: Write a MockDataset that satisfies the JsonlDataset interface (has .path, .num_tokens, __len__, __getitem__) without real file I/O; __getitem__ returns a synthetic DataItem (input_ids list of zeros with length matching num_tokens[i])

Step 3: Implement the 8-process distributed test class inheriting DistributedTestBase; each rank independently constructs CustomPackDataset with mmap=True; record init wall-clock time and peak RSS (psutil) + tracemalloc peak before/after __init__

Step 4: In the distributed test, each rank calls __getitem__ on its shard of pack indices (rank-strided); record total __getitem__ wall-clock time and average per-item latency; assert no crashes and results are non-empty

Step 5: Add an assertion or logged comparison between mmap=True and mmap=False memory usage to validate the mmap memory saving; run the stress test and ensure it completes within a reasonable time bound
